steps.prompt_snapshot:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 1
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['transmute']
Prompt family: transmute
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Reformulate the provided problem into a precise, actionable mathematical challenge. Your goal is to strip away all conversational filler and administrative metadata, leaving only a rigorous statement of the objective, the constraints, and the operational rules. Instead of describing an algorithm or a general strategy, provide a clean, structured version of the problem that defines exactly what needs to be calculated. If the problem contains variables or data that are not explicitly listed in the text, represent them as clearly defined symbolic inputs (e.g., 'Given a grid G...', 'Given a set of stamps S...') so that the logical relationship between the inputs and the objective is unmistakable. The resulting reformulation should be a direct, high-density version of the problem that is ready for immediate logical or computational processing.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S5-ahc032-mod-stamp
Title: S5. AHC032 / Mod Stamp

Problem:
Choose up to K = 81 placements of 3 x 3 stamps on a 9 x 9 board to maximize the sum of board entries modulo 998244353.

Grading target:
Score is the sum of final cell values modulo 998244353.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

authored_response.decision_notes: Reformulated the problem into a formal optimization statement.
authored_response.message_for_next_agent: Given a 9x9 grid B where each cell (i, j) contains an initial integer value v_{i,j} (for 1 <= i, j <= 9), and a set of possible 3x3 stamp placements P = {(r, c) | 1 <= r, c <= 7}, where each placement p in P covers the cells {(i, j) | r <= i < r+3, c <= j < c+3}. Let S be a subset of chosen placements such that |S| <= 81. Define the final value of each cell (i, j) as f(i, j) = v_{i,j} + sum_{p in S} I((i, j) in p), where I is the indicator function. Objective: Find a subset S that maximizes the objective function: (sum_{i=1}^{9} sum_{j=1}^{9} f(i, j)) mod 998244353

messages.content:
Reformulate the provided problem into a precise, actionable mathematical challenge. Your goal is to strip away all conversational filler and administrative metadata, leaving only a rigorous statement of the objective, the constraints, and the operational rules. Instead of describing an algorithm or a general strategy, provide a clean, structured version of the problem that defines exactly what needs to be calculated. If the problem contains variables or data that are not explicitly listed in the text, represent them as clearly defined symbolic inputs (e.g., 'Given a grid G...', 'Given a set of stamps S...') so that the logical relationship between the inputs and the objective is unmistakable. The resulting reformulation should be a direct, high-density version of the problem that is ready for immediate logical or computational processing.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S5-ahc032-mod-stamp
Title: S5. AHC032 / Mod Stamp

Problem:
Choose up to K = 81 placements of 3 x 3 stamps on a 9 x 9 board to maximize the sum of board entries modulo 998244353.

Grading target:
Score is the sum of final cell values modulo 998244353.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

message.content:
```json
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "Reformulated the problem into a formal optimization statement.",
  "message_for_next_agent": "Given a 9x9 grid $B$ where each cell $(i, j)$ contains an initial integer value $v_{i,j}$ (for $1 \le i, j \le 9$), and a set of possible $3 \times 3$ stamp placements $P = \{(r, c) \mid 1 \le r, c \le 7\}$, where each placement $p \in P$ covers the cells $\{(i, j) \mid r \le i < r+3, c \le j < c+3\}$. \n\nLet $S \subseteq P$ be a subset of chosen placements such that $|S| \le 81$. \n\nDefine the final value of each cell $(i, j)$ as $f(i, j) = v_{i,j} + \sum_{p \in S} \mathbb{I}((i, j) \in p)$, where $\mathbb{I}$ is the indicator function. \n\nObjective: Find a subset $S$ that maximizes the objective function:\n$$\left( \sum_{i=1}^{9} \sum_{j=1}^{9} f(i, j) \right) \pmod{998244353}$$"
}
```

```json
}
```
